name: Spicy Beef Tostadas
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Zucchini, cut into thin strips (2 cups)
4 Green onions, bias cut into 1-inch lengths
1/2 cup Monterey Jack cheese, shredded (2 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Partially freeze the beef. Slice across the grain into thin strips (bite-size); set aside.
2. Spray six 10-ounce custard cups with nonstick spray coating.
3. Brush tortillas lightly with warm water to soften. Gently press them into the cups. Bake at 350°F for 12 to 15 minutes. Remove from cups and set aside.
4. For sauce, combine salsa, cornstarch, and bouillon; set aside.
5. Spray a large skillet with nonstick coating. Preheat over high heat.
6. Cook and stir zucchini over high heat for 1 1/2 minutes. Add the onions; cook and stir for another 1 1/2 minutes or until the vegetables are crisp-tender. Remove from skillet.
7. Place oil in hot skillet. Cook and stir half of the beef for 2 to 3 minutes. Remove cooked beef and cook the other half. Return all of the beef to skillet.
8. Stir sauce into the skillet and add it to the skillet. Cook and stir until the mixture is thick and bubbly. Continue cooking for 2 more minutes.
9. Stir in the vegetable mixture. Evenly divide the meat mixture among the tortilla cups and top with cheese. Serve immediately.
10. Makes 6 servings.
